TextENGNew: ...She still hasn't woken up?
With a sigh, the doctor shook his head, eyes lowered. On the bed beside him, Satoko slept on with a peaceful face.
changeReason: Changed Irie's title from one role to another (JPN: 監督); Changed Irie's title from 'coach' to 'doctor' (JPN: 監督 can mean coach/director)

TextENGNew: For the past few days, it had become our routine to stop by the clinic after school and ask about Satoko's condition.
But no matter when we came, Satoko never woke up. All we could do was hold her hand, wipe her down, and tell sleeping Satoko about what happened at school.
Here, a change of clothes.
Shion, don't push yourself too hard, okay? It's fine to stay overnight in this room, but at least make sure you get proper sleep.
I'm fine. I do keep myself in check, in moderation. It's just... when I think about teasing Satoko first thing when she wakes up, I can't fall asleep... tee-hee.
With a shrug, Shion gave a wry smile.
She looked a little drawn, with faint dark shadows under her eyes.
...Even if she's skipping school, running back and forth between Angel Mort, preparing the shrine food stalls, and coming to the clinic has to be rough on her mind as well as her body.
If you looked closely, exhaustion showed plainly on the doctor's face too.
changeReason: Changed Irie's title from one role to another (JPN: 監督); Changed Irie's title from 'coach' to 'doctor' (JPN: 監督 can mean coach/director)

TextENGNew: According to Shion, ever since then he'd been pulling all-nighters day after day, constantly working on blood tests in hopes of improving Satoko's condition.
On top of his regular duties, he was doing this much for her. We couldn't help feeling deeply grateful to him.
It's such a shame... I thought we'd all get to make a huge fuss together at today's festival...
Rena's shoulders fell in disappointment. Then Rika-chan took her hand and gave her a bright nipah smile.
There will be a festival next year too. If we sit here looking gloomy, Satoko will definitely worry when she wakes up.
Rika-chan...
...Yeah, Rika-chan's right. Let's go all out and enjoy today's festival, then report back to Satoko with souvenirs in hand! That's how we'll do it!
All right, I'm on board with that, Kei-chan! Shion, you're one of today's stars too, right? It's the big day for the Sonozaki family shops, so I won't forgive you if you show me a gloomy face!
Who are you calling gloomy?! I'm not you, Onee.
...Haaah, well, Kei-chan does have a point.
Then brace yourself, Kei-chan, okay? If you dawdle, I'll snatch every last customer from that fire-sale auction of yours!
Yeah, bring it on! You're the one who better not end up crying later, got it?
Ahahahaha! It's a one-on-one showdown between Keiichi-kun and Shii-chan! I wonder which one will win... I wonder?
Oh, I pick Kei-chan, no questions asked. Hope Shion's stall is dead empty and goes into the red, huh?
Mion... aren't you saying the exact opposite of what you just said?
Then I'll stay here and watch over Satoko-chan. If she wakes up, I'll call the shrine festival headquarters.
Please take good care of things, Director. For now, if you need to call me, just say "the prettier Sonozaki sister," and I'll be there at mach speed.
changeReason: Changed Irie's title from 'coach' to 'director' (JPN: 監督)

TextENGNew: Oh, and the grubby one means my sister.
Who're you calling grubby? What am I, kitchen garbage?!
If anything, please call her non-burnable garbage.
Rika-chan... Mii-chan is environmentally friendly, you know?
Rena... you're going about this all wrong...
And so, we asked the Director to stay behind and headed to the festival grounds.
changeReason: Changed Irie's title from 'coach' to 'director' (JPN: 監督)

TextENGNew: Just like Rika-chan said, there wasn't much we could do even if we stayed by Satoko's side. And if she found out we couldn't enjoy the festival because of her, it would only hurt Satoko instead.

In that case... we'd make memories fun enough to make her jealous, and tell her all about them when she woke up. So that even just hearing about it would cheer her up, enough to make her look forward to next year's Watanagashi...!
The shrine grounds were already packed with people.

In yukata and in everyday clothes, all kinds of faces wandered from stall to stall. Young people, the elderly, couples, families, tight-knit groups of friends. Some faces I knew, and plenty I was seeing for the first time.
And among them... huh, that girl?
Oh? Heeey, Natsumi-chaaaan!!
Mion noticed her right away too and gave her a big wave. The girl turned at the call, then came running over with a bright smile.
Mion-chan, good evening! I knew I'd run into you~!
Glad you made it, Natsumi-chan! You here with your family today?
Yeah!
As she said that, Natsumi-chan waved back toward the group behind her, a mixed crowd of men and women, young and old, probably her relatives.
When a woman who seemed to be her mother smiled and said, 'We're going on ahead~!', she nodded energetically and turned back to us.
This girl was Kimiyoshi Natsumi-chan. She was a relative of Mayor Kimiyoshi, and Mion had introduced us before at Seven Mart.
Last time we talked, she said she wanted to come to this Watanagashi again... and it looked like she managed it.
...Huh? The person next to Mion-chan... could it be?
Natsumi... from the Kimiyoshi family? Ahahaha, it's been so long~!
Huh? Then it really is you, Shion-chan? Kyaa~~, what happened? Did you come back to Hinamizawa?!
Well, a lot happened, actually... My sis started crying that she'd be lonely by herself, so I had no choice...
Don't lie, you liar!
Ahahaha~~, you two are exactly the same as always~~!
With Natsumi-chan between them, Shion teased while Mion got all worked up. Natsumi-chan looked back and forth between them with nostalgia, laughing in delight.
...Hey, I was thinking. Around here, doesn't a normal girl stand out more?
......So that means Rena isn't normal either? ...Is she~?
I-it huwts, huwts! R-Rena, my cheeksh are gonna rip off!!
...Sorry to keep everyone waiting. ...Mii?
Aaah, it's Rika-chan~~!
Bwoh?!
The moment Rika-chan finally arrived in time, changed into her shrine maiden outfit, Rena instantly switched into take-home mode... and shoved me aside.
Hauuuuu~~! M-miko-outfit Rika-chan is suuuper cuuute~~!! I-I'm taking her hooomeee~~!!
M-miiii~~?! R-Rena, I can't breathe~~!
Rika-chan... from the Furude family? It's been a while--
Wait, Mion-chan?
...You should stay back for now. It'll shave years off your life.
After chatting for a while, Natsumi-chan went back to her family. Apparently she's acting as a guide for her friend group.
While we were at it, we invited her to join the club activity we're about to start, but... the braided older girl politely turned us down for her.
According to her, it was something like, We're about to take on a veeery important mission to cheer on two people in love~!
I had no clue what that was supposed to mean, but Rena, Shion, Rika-chan... even Mion were nodding along like, yeah, yeah.
...What does that mean?
Hey there, Rika-chan and Keiichi-kun! So you two came too.
A-Akasaka-san!
When I turned at the voice calling out to me, Akasaka-san was there.
Beside him stood a beautiful woman, probably his wife, and an adorable little girl.
Hm...? Speaking of adorable...?
H-hauuuuuuhhh!!! Who? Who is this girl?! She's cuuute, unbelievably cuuute!!
...Eh? K-kyahhh?!
R-Rena?! W-wait a second!!
Shut up! Be quiet! Be positive!!
This girl's mine~!! Haaauuuhhh, I'm taking her home with meee!!
I-I told you! What are you doing to my Miyuki...
Guhblegh?!
A-Akasaka?!
...Akasaka-san rushed in to get back the girl called Miyuki, and Rena turned the tables on him beautifully.
Uh... if I remember right, Akasaka-san is an active Public Security detective, isn't he? Just what is Rena, to brush him aside in a single blow??
...More importantly, this is definitely obstruction of official duties. Mii.
Huh? Then this person is police?
...If he can be taken down by a schoolgirl, Japan's public safety doesn't inspire much confidence.
...Though I'm not sure it's fair to put Rena in Cute Mode in the category of "schoolgirl."
If anything, "monster" fits better, don't you think?
F... fwehh...?! B-big sis, I-I can't breathe...!!
Anyway, all of us had to pile on to restrain Rena, who was running wild... no, on the verge of exploding, and we finally managed to free the girl... Miyuki-chan.
After that, maybe getting KO'd by a girl shocked him badly... Akasaka-san headed toward the first-aid tent with his wife, shoulders slumped.
And Miyuki-chan...
Big sis, you're strong~! Miyuki wants to get strong too! Teach me how I can become strong enough to beat even Papa, okay♪
Ahahahaha, of course that's fine~! The trick is to twist your hips first, and when you throw your fist, put your weight into it and drive your wrist in like you're gouging...
...In no time at all, she'd taken to Rena, and the two had built a very misguided master-disciple relationship.
Or rather... what am I supposed to make of two girls teaching each other martial arts right in the middle of the festival square?!
And on top of that, Akasaka-san's wife entrusted her with, 'Please raise her to be a strong girl,' so Rena's fired-up enthusiasm is on a whole different level...!
Still, even after going through all that, she's either innocent or just plain gutsy... she's got promise.
...For some reason, I feel like that girl and I are destined to become sworn enemies.
Well... Rena and Miyuki-chan both seem happy, so I guess it's fine.
Even so, I need to keep a close watch so this doesn't get carried back home.
...And with that, let's get started! The customary Watanagashi festival game, and with six participants, it's the Six-Fierce Battle Royale!!
Oooooohhhhhh!!!
...Huh? Miyuki can join too?
Having been added to the count before she even noticed, Miyuki-chan just stared blankly, eyes wide.
...Well, that's the normal reaction. But to Mion, grand prize winner of the 'World's Worst at Reading the Room' award, that's only a trivial detail!
Congratulations, Miyuki-chan! In honor of the good fortune that brought you to Hinamizawa today, I grant you the privilege of a one-day club activity trial!!
Club activity... what's that?

Excellent question! Our club is, in short, one that takes a sharp scalpel to the complexities of modern society, finding value in the practical and concrete, and sparing no effort in endless study and pursuit...
As always, Mion's explanation makes no sense. I mean, would a little girl understand something that complicated?
My condolences, but now that you've come back to Hinamizawa, you'll be taking home the best... and the worst memories...! Heh heh heh!
Following that up, Shion licked her lips in a dark, deep mood and flashed a grin.
...Rena is weak... so please don't bully me. Let's all get along, okay!
And even while she looked up with teary eyes... a pale blue flame burned deep within Rena's gaze. She's good, frighteningly good. And utterly terrifying...!
Umm, then... what should Miyuki do?
Miyuki-chan still has no idea what's going on, but... the fact that she's standing in this terrifying atmosphere without crying is honestly amazing.
...In other words, it's a club where everyone plays games together.
As expected, only Rika-chan gave an explanation that really hit the mark... but at this point, who would believe something like that?
...Look at it the other way, and it could also mean, 'Let's play with Miyuki instead of Satoko,' though.
And she says something outrageous that casually and fresh-faced!
Miyuki-chan... hey, um...?!

I figured it really was cruel to drag an innocent little girl into this, and I was about to tell her to hurry back to her parents... when I felt several terrifying presences at my back, and a chill ran down my spine.
When I turned around... the four of them were still silent, but they were pressing down on me with sharp glares alone.
And their eyes were saying it... Are. You. Taking. Her. Place?
Huh? What's wrong, Onii-chan?
N-no... i-it's nothing. ...S-sorry...
Sorry, Miyuki-chan... I don't want to be the sacrifice either...
For now, we'll keep it simple and use a point system! Winner and loser are decided by total score at the end!
Well, those are pretty standard rules.
And last place gets a penalty game!!
That's way too obvious!!
Is there no consideration for beginners here?!
Now then, Club President? Give us the starting call~!
...Sheesh. Alright, here we go! Six-Fiend Brawwwl!!
The battle across the festival stalls that began with my declaration turned into such an uproar it drew the attention of almost everyone at the festival.
Takoyaki speed-eating, shaved-ice chugging, ring toss, shooting gallery, and all sorts of other things...

It was only kids horsing around, nothing special in itself, but each of us was dead serious about winning, so fired up and enjoying it so deeply that even the people watching got swept up in it, and the excitement surged.
Miyuki-chan, joining for the first time, was bewildered at first and flustered with little au-au noises, but little by little she found her own pace and had a wonderful time.
More than that, in the shooting-gallery match she did well enough to get one over on us, to the point that even Rena said, As expected of Rena's number-one apprentice! Hau~☆
Rika-chan too, since she rarely got to run around and play this much with a younger girl, completely slipped into a big-sister role and looked after her.
In fact, when Akasaka-san came to pick her up, she cried instead, saying, I wanna play together more~!, and Rena watched her off with real tears in her eyes...
...Maebara-kun! It's about time, so I'm counting on you~?
Okaaay, got it! Leave it to me!
Oh, Kei-chan, it's finally your turn, huh? I'm looking forward to seeing the silver-tongued magician at full power.
...Couldn't you cheer me on with something better? What's with this silver-tongued magician stuff? At least call me 'K the Magician'!
...That makes you sound even shadier. What are you, some tea-loving commander?
Ahahahaha! Well then, Kei-chan, I'm heading to the parlor stall.
If we win the sales showdown, you have to buy me one of those French dolls too, like the one you gave Onee before, okay?
Wait! I could give that one without hesitation because I got it for free, but buying one is a whole different problem! Those things are expensive, you know?!
Oho~? Kei-chan, I don't like the way you said that~.
...Then if Kei-chan loses, I'll demand an even bigger present for myself!
Wh-?!
E-even bigger... w-what would that be? What would it be? Could it be... an engagement ring??
Oooooooohhh~~~!!!
Cheers erupt from all around us. ...Hey, this has nothing to do with you people, does it?!
Ooooh~~? A public confession of love right here~? You two are really fired up~!
N-no, that's not it?! I-I-I-I d-d-don't m-mean it like th-that?!
...Mii just self-destructed, nano desu. Mii~.
Hauuu~, I wonder what kind of ring it'd be, hau hau~! A diamond? A ruby? Or maybe orichalcum?!
That last one doesn't even exist! At least say platinum, are you trying to forge a legendary weapon?! ...And anyway, there's no way a student like me can buy something like thattt!
So with that, this contest is going to have a major impact on Kei-chan's household finances from now on. Do your best, 'Onii-san'♪
Hey, wait! Don't go deciding that on your ownnn!
And just like that, the bargain auction started with me shoved straight to the brink.
At first, the pressure and embarrassment made me trip up and fumble here and there.
But it was so much fun seeing people burst out laughing at my calls and sales patter, or stop in their tracks and peer in with keen interest, that from there on out I was in my usual element.

And as I gradually got used to it, I found my rhythm, smoothly recommending items one after another and selling them off; before I knew it, what had seemed like a huge stock of goods was sold out in no time.
Meanwhile, Shion's parlor stall was a massive hit too.
Just as expected, even customers who had known about Angel Mort but never quite managed to go inside took this chance, marveled at how good the cakes were, and ate with delight.
Above all, the low-priced crepe-roll cakes meant for children sold out in an instant.
The manager beamed at the turnout, far beyond expectations, and happily said he wanted to reflect this huge success in future product plans.
Which only made it all the more regrettable that Satoko couldn't join us this time...
When she wakes up, we'll definitely tell her all about how much fun it was.
And next year, we'll make it even livelier, so Satoko can keep smiling the whole time. ...No one said it aloud, but we all made the same vow in our hearts.
Me too, of course.
Unbelievable.
No... maybe I just didn't want to believe it. Even now, while I ran, part of me still prayed this had to be some kind of mistake.
Blending into the darkness, I ran through the mountain path, guided only by moonlight and the faint flow of the wind.
My pursuers tried to find me with blinding searchlights, but their technique was painfully amateur.
Used like that, the lights only made it easier for the one fleeing to see the way, while the reflected glare got in the pursuers' own way.
No wonder Okonogi complains... I muttered that, then gave a wry smile at how out of place the thought was.
...I'm hardly in any position to say that myself...
After all, I had gotten too deeply wrapped up in Takano-san and Irie's relationship, and forgotten my true duty: to keep watch.
And this disgrace is the result... I was bitterly frustrated by my own softness.
...Public Security's intervention, and the opaque flow of funds into the Irie Clinic.
I had thought I was only casually looking into a passing question from Rika-chan... but what I reached was a shocking truth, too great to be contained by the single word astonishment.
Unsettling movements within 'Tokyo.' And here in Hinamizawa, movements that should have been absolutely impossible. ...And Takano Miyo.
When I learned that truth... at first I thought it had to be a mistake. It looked as though Takano-san had chosen a road to ruin, and by common sense, it could only be called treason.
But... then I changed my point of view.
changeReason: Old uses 'he changed his perspective'; new uses 'I changed my point of view' -- different narrative person (third vs first), affecting who the narrator is

TextENGNew: What if this act of destroying the plan was being used in a power struggle among 'Tokyo's' upper ranks? And what if Takano-san knew that, and on top of that was receiving orders from one of those power holders?
...Then it all connected. Every event that had seemed nothing but inexplicable fit cleanly into place, like puzzle pieces locking together.
And what emerged was... the 'ruin' of all Hinamizawa. A grand 'festival' Takano Miyo had drawn up as an offering to god.
--Kill me? ...Do you think you can, Jirou-san...?
...As I ran along the dark, silent mountain road, her voice lingering at my ear kept repeating, echoing deep in my mind...
Just before the Watanagashi offering was to begin.
Takano-san had invited me to sneak a look inside the Saiguden while no one was watching, and the two of us made our way to the back of Furude Shrine's grounds.
changeReason: Changed between 'Saiguden' (Japanese term) and 'ritual storehouse' (English translation) for 祭具殿

TextENGNew: In front of it, I turned to her, her eyes glittering with excitement, and decided to ask her point-blank.
Takano-san. I want you to answer me honestly. What are you plotting?
Plotting...? What's wrong, Jirou-san? Why ask something so strange all of a sudden?
I've had people look into what you've been up to lately. ...Takano-san, that woman you were talking to on the phone the other day... who was she?
Who, you ask...? I talk to plenty of women on the phone. Just who are you referring to?
She kept pretending not to know. ...Even so, the moment our eyes met, I caught the unconscious way she dodged my gaze, and I realized I'd struck home and rattled her.
...Don't play dumb. I've already confirmed that a certain faction at Tokyo headquarters has been making strange moves lately. And that you've been in contact with part of it.
......Hmm... As expected, you're quite capable. I've fallen for you all over again. ...Hee-hee!

Takano-san brushed back her hair and shot me a sidelong glance, and I involuntarily caught my breath. ...I knew that gesture was meant to look down on me and provoke me, but... that uncanny allure was also why I'd been drawn to her.
Hey, Jirou-san. ...I love you.
...Don't change the subject.
...I've been with all kinds of men, but ambition and wildness never suited me. A stiff, serious man like you was the one who fit me best.
So I want you to accept all of me. I want you to understand everything about the real Takano Miyo...
...What is it you want me to understand?
At that question, Takano-san gave a soft laugh and shrugged.

...She was so bewitchingly beautiful it sent a chill through me, and her cruel eyes gleamed eerily. For an instant, I thought I could see my own reflection in them, and I forced strength into my legs to keep from stepping back.
...It's finally time. Very soon now... The day my dream... the dream I've watched for so long, comes true, is almost here. Hee-hee-hee...
A dream... ?
Yes, Jirou-san. Haven't I told you before? To obtain power equal to a god, become an existence on par with one, reign over this world, and pass divine judgment on foolish, powerless humans...
Hee-hee-hee... Wonderful, isn't it? And it will soon come to pass. By my own hand...!

Unable to keep up with Takano-san's intoxicated speech, I could only stare at her in stunned silence. But then I suddenly came to my senses and realized the conversation had gone off track, and though it felt boorish, I cut in and interrupted her.
...What you’re trying to do is a mass of self-deception and self-intoxication. From my position in oversight, I cannot permit it. ...So I’m asking you. Reconsider now, and stop this plan.
...Your position in oversight, hm... Then what about you yourself?
...What?
We were unilaterally told to end the Hinamizawa research within three years. And the reason was to line their own pockets... it was nothing but their ego.
I have no intention of excessively glorifying my research... or my grandfather’s, and parading it around.

Even so... after believing in people’s goodwill, after wearing myself down with the hope that someday it would be rewarded, is it arrogance to think you would understand how I feel... that at least you, and only you, would understand?
Takano-san...
Jirou-san. ...I can’t stop anymore. Just like a locomotive rolling downhill, once it starts moving, it can’t be stopped. I have staked everything I am on the study of Hinamizawa Syndrome.

That was crushed by the ego of scum who think of nothing but money, and now I’m empty. If that’s the case, then at least in the end... it wouldn’t be unfair for me to have a chance to fulfill my own dream, would it?
...And for that, you’ll use countless lives as stepping-stones?
...In the end, people are always alone. If it satisfies their desires, they can throw justice and ethics into the gutter without blinking. History proves that... doesn’t it?
And the ones who leave their names in history are mostly people like that. Honest people get made into fools... ahaha, they really said it well, didn’t they!
...Takano-san, calm down and think it over. You... at least the you I know, shouldn’t be this kind of person.
Oh my... hee-hee-hee! Jirou-san, I really do love that naïve side of you, you know? But sadly, this is what I’ve always been. I’ve drunk far too much blood; these hands are drenched in it.
Once you’ve known that smell, that touch, that taste, there’s no going back. Not that I have any intention of going back... hee-hee-hee!
Takano-san...?!
At that moment, the trees all around rustled unnaturally. Then came damp breathing, clearly different from the sound of the wind.
N-no way, Takano-san...?!
...Hey, Jirou-san. I like you very much. If you quietly cooperate, then of course your reward can be anything you want.
If that’s what you want, I can give you all of me, and all the time I have left. ...Just as I told you about myself and only you, that much is my honest feeling.
So you can take your time. ...Will you think it over?
.........I’m sorry, but there’s only one answer. I can’t let your group... let you, go...!
...I see, just as I thought. What a pity...
Truly... what a pity!!
Kei-chan, here you go.
Oh, thanks.
Taking the clump of cotton Mion handed me, I crouch by the riverbank and gently set it adrift.
On the river, lit by the faint dusk, countless cotton puffs drifted along, giving off a strangely dreamlike air.
...This is nice, stuff like this.
At my offhand murmur, Mion answered in a gentle voice, Yeah. Then she knelt beside me and squeezed my hand tight.
...Good work, Kei-chan. Thanks to you, it was a huge success.
It's thanks to everyone. I couldn't have done anything on my own.
Even so, the reason it turned out this lively is because you were here, Kei-chan. Everybody knows that. Me too, of course.
Peering into my face, Mion smiled as she said it. Hearing those words, the fatigue and pressure from all the running around these past few weeks felt like they were blown away in an instant.
...Hey, Kei-chan.
What is it?
...Um... don't carry it all by yourself, okay?
In silence, I stare at the water's surface. Mion tightened her grip on my hand, then leaned against me, resting her cheek softly on my shoulder.
...I heard a little from Shion. That you've been going around investigating something really big, Kei-chan. Together with Rika-chan...
Is it something you can't tell even us?
...Sorry.
That was all I could say.
The conspiracy lurking behind Hinamizawa that we're chasing. And Takano-san's true identity.

...If only I could just come clean and ask for help, how much easier and more reassuring it would be. In truth, I've lost count of how many times I've thought how much it'd help to have Mion with us in this situation.
Even so.
The enemy is cunning, and powerful besides. As long as we move in a small group, we're harder to detect, but the more allies we add, the more wary they become, and the bigger our own openings grow.
That could just end up dragging everyone in irresponsibly...
So I still couldn't say it. With so little information, this still wasn't the time to confess.
...Yeah, okay. I'll trust you.
Mion...
I turned to look at Mion. She wore a gentle, wonderfully warm smile.
If you're keeping it secret, Kei-chan, it's because you're thinking of us, right? Then I won't ask anything.
No matter what happens, I'll trust the way you chose, Kei-chan, and I'll tell anyone with my head held high that you're right.
...Sorry.
--But, Kei-chan, promise me. If you need help, don't ever hold back. I'm always on your side, and I mean to be your best ally. ...Okay?
...Yeah, I got it.
At that, Mion nodded brightly with a cheerful All right!, stood up, and brushed the dirt from her knees. Then she gave my shoulder a solid thump and flashed me an encouraging smile.
I'm counting on you, Chairman-san! See you.
With that, Mion gave a little wave and ran off toward the group of old folks gathered beyond the riverbank. ...I was grateful for her consideration, and felt sorry all the same.
--Keiichi.
A voice called me from behind. When I turned around, sure enough, it was Rika-chan.
Did Mii say anything?
No. But it seemed like she noticed about us. ...Until we say it ourselves, she's probably pretending not to know.
...If we move too much, Takano and the others might move their plan forward. It may be passive, but I think now is the time to wait for Akasaka and Tomitake to act.
...Yeah.
We kept staring at the water. The drifting cotton tufts had long since gone downstream, and the river had returned to how it was.
The flow of the river never ceases, and yet it is never the same water... huh.
...What is that?
It's from Hojoki. An essay from long, long ago, I guess. It means time keeps flowing without end, but not a single event within it is ever exactly the same.
...Is that so?
Rika-chan said that listlessly, then gave a lonely smile.

I've lived through the same span of time thousands of times now, so many that I've forgotten the exact number. ...But the more I repeated it, the more it became the same thing over and over, and I've grown thoroughly sick of it.
When people say fate has infinite possibilities... I think that's just idle talk from those who've never lived through it.
Maybe so. But... isn't that because you've kept choosing the same options, Rika-chan?
...You're awfully quick to decide things for me. What do you know about me, Keiichi?
Looking taken aback, Rika-chan raised her face and spoke.
Do you think I'd want something so boring as the same choice over and over?
Even joy and surprise stop being interesting if they happen twice in a row. That's why I always looked forward to something different happening. I was always chasing the unexpected.
...But, Keiichi, fate's branches are not infinite. I learned that... and that's why...
That sounds like your own assumption, Rika-chan... isn't it?
I kept speaking to Rika-chan.
You said before this world was something you'd never experienced, right, Rika-chan? Then fate hasn't run dry yet.
The possibilities may be finite, but it's too soon to think you've found them all. So let's keep trying a little longer!
...Even if all we can see ahead is a dead end?
Who decided that? Not fate, not God - wasn't it you, Rika-chan? So let's believe there are still choices left.
.........You're right. I think I'll take that gamble. Because this is probably the last time...
I gave a wry smile to myself. It couldn't be helped, but Rika-chan still couldn't shake the habit of speaking in a tone of desperation.
If only we could find that girl Rika-chan said had always been by her side... Hanyuu... things might change a little.
...And then.
At that moment, the moon slipped out from between the clouds, lighting things a little. ...As Rika-chan stared vaguely at the flowing river, I heard her catch her breath.
Hm? What's wrong, Rika-chan...?
.... No, that can't...! Th-then... the me who's here right now, could it be...?!
Rika-chan... hey, Rika-chan?!
Ah... aaahhh...!!
She trembled...
Her eyes were wide with shock, clear even in the darkness, her body shaking... Rika-chan clutched her head and staggered backward.
Then, after about three steps back, she failed to notice a large stone behind her and fell on her backside with a short yelp.
Huh?! Are you all right, Rika-chan?!
Can you stand? I hope you're not hurt...
Rika-chan stared blankly at the hand I'd rushed over to offer her... For some reason, she froze with her own hand half-extended, as if unsure whether to take it.
...Her small back was trembling in tiny spasms. Her face seemed to have gone pale, her eyes unfocused.
Are you really okay, Rika-chan? The coach should still be around, so I'll go get him right—
M-...meep, I'm fine. I'll just go home and rest qui— ...?!
Suddenly.
Rika-chan sprang to her feet as if jolted by something... and turned sharply toward the mountains behind us, eyes narrowed.
Then she held her breath, swallowing hard, and scanned the surroundings with vigilant eyes, as though sensing some presence.
And then—.
...!! Tomitake is in danger...!!
Huh?!
Come with me, Keiichi! Tomitake is being chased!!
...How much time had passed since then?

Fortunately, I'd managed to flee into the mountains before the Mountain Dogs could seize me. But the pursuers had gradually tightened their net, and by now every escape route was completely sealed off.
changeReason: Changed plural 'mountains' to singular 'mountain' matching JPN 山

TextENGNew: Worse still, the festival fireworks served as cover for their gunfire. They weren't firing in bursts, but they'd already shot at me several times, and I'd barely managed to dodge each one.
Escape might no longer be possible.
If so, then at least—.
At least... at the very least, for Rika-chan...!
With only that thought driving me, I ran through the hills and woods, desperately searching for a way out. But—
In despair, I stopped the legs that had been moving so frantically moments ago.
Spread out before me—
...was a swamp.
Tch... Is this the end of the line...?!
—?! What was that sound...?!
We'd been running up the dark mountain trail by the feeble light of a small flashlight... when partway up, we clearly heard a tremendous splash from the direction of the valley.
If I remembered right, a little way downhill from here... there was a small swamp. I'd never checked how deep it was, but the adults had warned us it was dangerous and off-limits.
And judging by the size of that splash... it was hard to believe it was just a stone or something falling in.
It sounded more like... a grown person had fallen in...?!
No, it can't...! Could Tomitake have...?!
Rika-chan doubled back down the path, rushed over to a steep cliff-like slope a short way behind us, and peered down from its edge.
...But even shining the flashlight, the darkness below was so deep we could hardly see a thing.
In fact, even where we stood the footing was treacherous—one careless moment and we could plunge headfirst into the valley.
T-...Tomitakeee!! Please... please answer me!!
C-careful, Rika-chan! Any farther and you'll really fall!
Even so, Rika-chan kept leaning out over the cliff trying to call down to him, and I desperately caught hold of her.
...Perhaps the contact finally brought her back to her senses. Rika-chan bit down on her lip with an anguished expression and crumpled to the ground, clutching her head.
...Is Tomitake-san down there?
I don't know... but I felt it! Tomitake was running through these mountains, and then...!
Bracing myself against a tree trunk, I stood up, switched off the flashlight as a precaution, and surveyed the area.
...Sure enough, just as Rika-chan said, several lights that were clearly not natural flickered between the trees, moving in irregular patterns.
Part of me wanted to go investigate... but we were essentially unarmed, and—Rika-chan aside—I wasn't used to navigating dark mountain trails at a run.
...There was nothing for it. Staying here any longer wouldn't accomplish anything.
Once I confirmed the lights were moving away, I knelt in front of Rika-chan and said, "Let's head back, Rika-chan."
We can't do anything just staying here. Let's go back and talk to Akasaka and the others. ...Is that all right, Rika-chan?
Mii... I understand. We'll make a tactical retreat for now.
With those words—
Just as Rika-chan rose to her feet, still wearing a look of reluctance—.
...Huh?
An instant.
Neither I nor Rika-chan could keep up with what was happening... We couldn't understand what had just occurred.
All there was... was the sensation of the ground crumbling away beneath her feet.
Rika-chan's small body floated into the air... and was swallowed into the darkness below.
By the time my thoughts started moving again... Rika-chan had vanished from before my eyes.
And then... and... then...?
The ground right in front of me... had collapsed... opening a gaping maw into the darkness...?!
Rika-...chan...? Rika-chan, Rika-chan...? —Rika-chaaaan!!!
I screamed until my voice went hoarse, but... nothing came back.
Only... silence... and an icy trembling and chill swept through my entire body, engulfing me...
......Target hit with stun gun. However, target fell into the swamp and was lost.
Hmph. ...Sloppy work. I know they can't use firearms, but I'd assumed they at least had experience with nighttime operations.
Takano swept her hair back with an exasperated look and addressed Okonogi, who stood behind her. She sensed him heave an apologetic sigh at her back, but she had no desire to turn and look.
``My apologies.@k@vS14/14/213400011. There's no way we could've expected he'd evade everyone and flee to the mountains.``
changeReason: Changed plural 'mountains' to singular 'mountain' matching JPN 山

TextENGNew: A soldier's duty is to respond to every conceivable contingency. Hard to believe these are handpicked members of the Self-Defense Forces, given this level of incompetence.
...That said—even without being allowed to fire, the fact that Tomitake had evaded that many men surrounding him, and even dodged the wired stun gun shots with near-miraculous agility...
Even Takano herself couldn't help being somewhat impressed.
...Nightingale 4 through 8, continue searching the swamp. 9 through 16, patrol the perimeter. Requesting additional orders for the remaining personnel.
You idiots! All personnel continue the mission until the target is found! What do you think this search party is for?! Get your act together!!
...Wait.

Takano paused, a sly grin spreading across her face as though an idea had struck her, and called out to stop Okonogi. ...That cold smile, paired with her black uniform, gave the impression of a witch—and Okonogi could not suppress an inward shudder.
If he's missing, then so be it. ...That 'contingency' we prepared just in case—why don't we go ahead and use it?
The 'contingency'...? But how do you intend to explain it to the 'Cuckoo'?

What we need is the elimination of the liaison and a public demonstration of Hinamizawa Syndrome. We can confirm his body later. As long as the 'curse' befalls someone else, there's no problem. ...We're running out of time. Hurry.
...It had been an 'insurance' policy, held in reserve in hopes that Tomitake might have a change of heart—but there was no reason not to use it now.
—With that thought, Takano issued the order with an especially ruthless expression. Okonogi nodded in silence, then took several men and left the room.
......There's no going back now, is there...? Hehe, hehehehe, ahahahahahahahahah!!!
...C-Commander. We have a problem...!
Hm? ...What's the matter? You look pale... Did you find something?
K-...keep this from the Major. According to Nightingale's report... R— Rika Furude... appears to have fallen into the same swamp as Tomitake...!
...Wh-what...?!
The search is still ongoing, but... her whereabouts are also unknown. They're requesting orders on how to proceed... urgently...
